titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the Julian J. Obermann Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 7, 2020 at 07:11

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, notebooks in English, German, and Hebrew, printed matter, and other materials of Julian Joel Obermann, professor of Semitic languages at Yale University.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the estate of Julian Obermann, 1959.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Obermann's notebooks in boxes 6-12 are open for research. Boxes 1-5 and 13-14 are unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access to the boxes 1-5, 13, or 14 should email mssa.assist@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Julian Joel Obermann Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Julian Joel Obermann (1888-1956): born in Warsaw, Poland; Ph.D., University of Vienna, 1915; emigrated to U.S. in 1923; professor of Semitic philology, Jewish Institute of Religion, New York, 1923-1931; professor at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, 1929-1930; professor of Semitic languages at Yale University, 1933-1956; author and editor of numerous books and articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, notebooks in English, German, and Hebrew, printed matter, and other materials of Julian Joel Obermann, professor of Semitic languages at Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Unsorted correspondence, postcards, photographs, minutes, and notes

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.

did.unittitle: Unsorted correspondence, postcards, photographs, and notebooks

accessrestrict.p: The material is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.

did.unittitle: Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts, photographs, and printed matter laid in)

Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts, photographs, and printed matter laid in)
Unsorted printed matter and photostats, with some manuscripts and photographs, notecards, blueprints, lantern slides

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.
